title: App development through Android Java
author: Priya
content:
INTERVIEW PREPS

All • Java • Spring • Android • PHP • English • HR • DevOps • Technical
Take Quiz

Android App Developement

  • Android App components
  • Device Independent Pixel
  • Intent
  • Intent-filter
  • Package Manager and Intent resolution
  • Content provider
  • Thread ,Looper ,Handler
  • AsyncTask
  • Activity Save State

Android App componets


There r 4 major components in android -
  1. Activity
  2. Services
  3. Broadcast Receiver
  4. Content Provider

1.Activity - It is an interactive screen which is visible to user in the frontend.It is a main component in app making without a activity or fragment we can't display any view to the user.
2.Services - An app component that can perform long running task in the background with no user interaction.

Types -
1.Bound services
2.started (unbound ) services
3.Intent services

Bound service - It is bound to activity or fragment when activity or fragment is shut down , there services stopped automatically by system.
Started service - Also a bound service , that can be bound to activity or fragemtns but htese services an survive between activities.ie… started from 1 activity and still be running in another activity process.
Intent service - They run in their own background threads and run long enough to accomplih a single task.
A bound service can be a started service but a started service can’t be a bound service.
If we create a service than we must create a separate thread for the service .Do not run the service in the main GUI thread..If we run the service in the main thread , it will show application not responding error..So, always create a separate thread for the service.


3.Broadcast Receiver - It's a class that generate an event after happening of certain task.It listens to the intent and deliver the event to the app when the action is needed to be done. Some live usecases r like - a message will be generated after booting process completes , when turning on/off airplane mode , Setting of alarm clock, Getting particular event reminders and notification on specific days of months.
Manifest.xml -
Example.java -> Public class Example extends BroadcastReceiver{ Public void onReceive(Context c, Intent i) { if(i.ACTION_BOOT_COMPLETED.equals(i.getAction())) Toast.makeatext(c ,”Boot Completed” ,Toast.Length_short).show(); if(ConnectivityManger.CONNECTIVITY_ACTION.equals(i.getAction())) Toast.makeatext(c ,”Connectivity changed” ,Toast.Length_short).show(); } }
4.Content Provider - It runs synchronously on the main thread and directly communicate with it while activity , services and broadcast receiver runs asynchronously on a queue and drop their message to the main thread. It is given high priority by the main thread [LOOPER] If continent provider request is under service by main thread than all other request of activity , service or broadcast receiver will be blocked.eg… Sometimes we see some error as Application not responding it is because the main thread is busy in performing some long running operations such as network access, db related queries etc .Hence , whole UI thread is blocked so no events can be dispatched .To avoid such errors , it is recommended not to perform any long running operation , or any network operation in the main thread.Use additional thread or service for such task having long running operations.
So, content provider do not wait in queue like other 3 components instead directly message looper.
Do not modify thread from UI thread , use service instead.
Services and content provider runs in separate threads.

Device Independent Pixel


Device independent or density independent means preserve the physical Size of our UI. If we give dimension as 100dp without thinking how big or small it would look like in different devices , than android takes care of how big or good our picture looks in all devices.System converts dp into pixel.If i am taking ldpi device than ,
For 100dp -> pixel = 100 *(120/160) 120 -> 120 dots per inch
Screen resolution - No. of pixel in screen.
Screen Density - No. of pixel in an inch.
lDpi(large) , mdpi ,hdpi , xhdpi(extra large)
No. of pixel will increase from ldpi to xhdpi
Dp - density pixel
SP- scalable pixel
Scaled pixel relates to font size of device not with pixel.
Ratio of Dp to pixel changes with screen density .

Intent


*Explicit Intent - We clearly specify the target component that may be an another activity or an external link directly .unlike in implicit intent where what data has to passed is shown but how that action will be performed is left for OS to decide on. Intent= new Intent(Activity1.class ,Activity2.class); startActivity(i);
*Implicit Intent - Tells what action to perform without worrying about who perform it. Whenever there is an event , an intent is created out of that and there is a broadcast receiver who actually listen to these events.
We don’t know source and destination but what we know is what action to perform .What is URI or data that we want to pass when particular action is performed.eg… To open a web page , u would like to open link www.google.com . To make a call , we r to transfer a phone no., to open a map , we transfer longitude and latitude.
There r many actions like - ACTION_CALL , ACTION_SEND etc.
ACTION_VIEW is the generalised form of all these actions.
THe purpose to open a browser or a call dialer , we use http or tel . They r called data scheme.As per data scheme , our action will start performing.
Different Intent Action
1.Call - Intent i =new Intent (Intent.ACTION_DIAL , Uri.parse(“tel:8933678990”)); startActivity(i);
2.GMap - Intent i =new Intent (Intent.ACTION_VIEW, Uri.parse(“geo:12.9778 , 77.39 ”)); startActivity(i);
3.Site - Intent i =new Intent (Intent.ACTION_VIEW , Uri.parse(“https://interviewprepforinsiders.blogspot.com”)); startActivity(i);
4.To see all the task perform by every app. Intent i =new Intent (Intent.ACTION_VIEW); startActivity(i);

4 pieces of intent
Action - Placing a phone call , giving sms ,email etc.
Data - What type of data we want to work with. Eg… To snap a photo from camera image will be out data ., URI , MIME Type
Extras - Providing some extra information.we give it in key-value pair.
Category - CATEGORY_HOME (We want our activity to start as soon our device boots up) , CATEGORY_LAUNCHER (We want our activity to appear in the system launcher . A shortcut is created for the activity to be displayed in the app drawer. , CATEGORY_BROWSABLE

Intent-filter


Intent-filter declaration is must in case of implicit intent.
Intent-filter - Our app respond to implicit intent through intent-filter defined inside manifest.xml containing some action , data and category.We can also create our custom intent-filter.
Action - At least 1 action must be defined and it can have any number of actions as desired.
Category - It is optional. If we want we may not defined any category and android always pass this test. But if 1 or more than 1 category r mentioned than for an intent to pass category test , than every category in the intent object must match a category in the intent-filter. Data - This element specifies a URI and a datatype(MIME type ie.. tel , mailTo). Data type passing is optional.
Implicit Intent of a Phone call Activity_main.xml -><button>

Package Manager and Intent resolution

Package Manager - It keeps an abstract information of every installed apps in our device.Apps package and permission , action it performs through intent-filter.
Intents r used to start an activity , service or a broadcast receiver.Package manager is responsible for handling intents.Whenever some intent-filter having some action,category or data is found in the manifest file.Package manager check those details and compare it with the list of activity ,services it has and if it finds none , it says “no app can perform this action” ..and if it finds more than 1 app who can perform this action it provides a list of all such apps for the user to choose from.

Package Manager with Broadcast receiver Whenever any system notification is generated like - battery low , network unavailable .So , such events is converted into intent object with a specific action ,category and data which is given to package manager.So, here package manager is going to call every broadcast receiver whose intent filter matches with the action, category , data of requested intent object.

Intent resolution - It works parallely with package manager to check the extra , data and action associated with an implicit intent and throws a list of all available apps who can perform those action.
resolveActivity() used incase of Intent resolution
Incase of implicit intent , it shows the number of apps that could perform implicit intent action.And sometimes it happens that on giving command startActivity(i) , our app gets crashed as there r no app to perform that particular action/function.Hence , to avoid crashing , write resolveActivity() to ensure that whether there is an app who can perform our action.

Eg… String s=”This is a sample message from priya”; Intent i = new Intent(); i.setAction(Intent.ACTION_SEND); i.setType(“text/plain”); i.putExtra(Intent.Extra_TEXT,s); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) ! =null) startActivity(i);

Content provider

Content provider allows to share info. Between 2 apps.
Content Resolver - A class that gives us the access to content provider. We r to create object of content resolver which gives us access to methods like - query() , insert(),update(),delete() etc.Object of content resolver calls or query content provider.Inside content provider , we give our SQLite DB. So, SQLite DB will be kept inside content provider.So, content provider act as a wrapper class around our DB.
Content URI - it makes a path between content resolver and provider. Content URI + permissions r required to fetch the data by content provider.
eg… we use
http:// - to access web page
file:// - to access file
content:// - to access content provider
Contract class gives us info. About all constant .ie… Content URI ,MIME type which r needed to access the data of the app.
MIME type = It is multipurpose internet mail extension developed to support FTP clients. app calls getType() to get MIME type from provider which tells the type of data.it can be web page or a json file.
Cursor - content resolver request content provider and in return provider responded by returning the cursor object as row or column.
Data stored in an app is private .So, content provider helps to get secure access to its data in form of cursor.

Thread ,Looper ,Handler

Thread ,Looper ,Handler If there r multiple messages on the main queue which r requested to be treated and delivered by looper.These messages if large in number forms message queue. The looper process these messages 1 by 1 . Looper transmit these messages to the handler.
Handler does 2 task -
  • Process the code or action inside the message.
  • Transmit these message or runnables back to main thread .

Handler does these 2 task simultaneously but using 2 different threads.

AsyncTask

Thread - They r individual running operations in a process or app. If we write all our code inside onCreate() than all the lines defined inside this would be called sequentially but we want certain part of our code to execute 1st before other.So , we can define that part inside another thread not in the main thread.
AsyncTask - Performs functionality in the background thread and publish the result to the main thread.Meant only for short operation purpose. If suppose we r using an app that can interact with server and i click on an icon to access some source code .Here, request after clicking the icon is sent to the server using some other thread T1 and we as a user still on our UI thread to perform dragging action on the screen .Now to achieve request over thread T1 ASyncTask class can be used.
AsyncTask overridden methods ->
onPreExecute() ,
onPostExecute(result) ,
doinBackground(Params) ,
onProgressUpdate(progress)

This class is used when the response of our request is to be received in a shorter duration.If duration is large Executors r used.And is generally used when we have some long running task.If we work on the main thread than all actions of views that should be performed instantly will go in the waiting stage bcoz main thread is already occupied doing something and would go unresponsive on further interaction with user.SO, always perform long running task in the BACKGROUND THREAD so that foreground operations r not affected.

Code - public class MainActivity extends AppCompatActivity{
onCreate{
Button b;
b.setOnClickListener(....){
UploadTask u = new UploadTask();
u.Preexecute(“Hi, this is priya”);
}
} }
Public class UploadTask extends AsyncTask{
Public void onPreExecute(String s){

Log.i(“Async by Priya”,s); // Hi , this is priya
Log.i(“Async by Priya”,Thread.currentThread().getName()); // aaaaaAsyncTask
}}

Activity Save State

- If suppose we have taken an editText in our activity but didn’t provide any id to it .SO on typing something over it in portrait mode if we rotate our screen to Landscape than the text written would get disappear on rotation of screen and that activity will get destroy .But after providing id to editText , the info typed remains there on rotation of screen. As we provide id , it automatically saved the info. So, on rotation in this case , activity will destroy itself and than would recreate on rotation with the same editText message that was given before rotation.
Inside view of how method calling takes place in an activity.
Take an activity having a dialog button , on click of which a dialog box opens.
  1. We we open our app = onCreate() -> onStart()->onREsume()
  2. When i clicked show dialog button = onPause()->onSaveInstanceState()
  3. On closing the dialog box = onResume()
  4. On clicking home button to come to the home screen = onPause()->onSaveInstanceState() ->onStop()
  5. Again going back to app = onRestart()->onStart()->onResume()
  6. As screen is rotated by pressing cntl +F11 = onPause()->onSaveInstanceState() ->onStop()->onDestroy()->onCreate()->onStart() -> onRestoreInstanceState() ->onResume()
  7. Again clicking showDialog = onPause()->onSaveInstanceState()
  8. On closing the dialog = onREsume()
  9. Again rotating the screen with cntl+F11 = onPause()->onSaveInstanceState() ->onStop()->onDestroy()->onCreate()->onStart()->onRestoreInstanceState() -> onResume()
  10. On clicking back button = onPause()->onStop()->onDestroy()


OnSaveInstanceState —- Activity’s object is held in memory when app is paused or stopped unless system explicitly destroys it.It store the info. Temporarily when our app is running.put() on bundle class is used to store info. Inside onSaveInstanceState() in the form of key-value pair. Always call super.onSaveInstanceState() to save view data in our activity. onSaveInstanceState(Bundle b){ b.put(“ “,” “) ; }
OnRestoreInstanceState() —- Once system recreates that activity after destroying it. onRestoreInstanceState() and onCreate() receive the same bundle. onCreate() can be used in place of onRestoreInstanceState(). Call super.onRestoreInstanceState() to restore view data in activity. onRestoreInstanceState(Bundle b){ b.put(“ “ , “ “ ); }

API & SDK

API allows 2 apps to communicate with each other and SDK allows APIs to use in the app..eg… We r making an app to capture the image of a person and this app connects to visual recognition service in the cloud to bring the name and other details of the captured image.SO, it’s all API that allows communication between 2 services or apps.SDK is like a toolkit that calls API for us.
API acts as a middleman between the frontend and the backend. eg…API is usually produced from the backend while frontend helps in consuming /handling request to these APIs.

startActivityForResult()

Used in the case like - when 2 activities run in the same time Real Use case - We make payment and when this action is passed by us , we want a request from server to give a result back to the payer as a confirmation to action Pay.So, getting result back to the main action as a confirmation , we use a callback() - onActivityResult().

.eg… Suppose we have 2 activity , on clicking openActivity2 , editText data from activity1 is passed to activity2 and on clicking add button result 5 is sent back to activity 1.

Code - Activity1.java
onCreate(){ int n1 = Integer.parseInt(e1.getText().toString()): int n2 = Integer.parseInt(e2.getText().toString()): Intent i = new Intent(Activity1.this , Activity2.class); i.putExtra(“n1”, n1); i.putExtra(“n2”, n2); startActivityForResult(i,1); }
Protected void onActivityResult(int request_code,int result_code ,Intent data){ if(request_code ==1){ if(result_code == RESULT_OK){ Int res = data.getIntExtra(“re”,0); text.setText(“ “+res); } if(result_code==RESULT_CANCELLED){ text.setText(“Nothing selected”); } }}}
Activity2.java
setTitle(“Activity1”); Intent i = getIntent(); Int a = i.getIntExtra(“n1”,0); Int b = i.getIntExtra(“n2”,0); TextView t = findViewById(R.id.tt); t.setText(“Number : “ +n1+”,”+n2); badd.setOnClickListener(....(){ Int result = a+b; Intent res =new Intent(); res.putExtra(“re”,res); setResult(RESULT_OK,res); finish(); } });
onActivityResult() - A method that listen for any activity intent results.

MediaPlayer

- To access audio or video file in the app , this class is used.
Syn - MediaPlayer m = MediaPlayer.create(this,R.raw.song);
m.start();
m.pause();
m.reset(); // To start the media file from beginning.

Web Service

Uses HTTP protocol and is a client server model.If client from his phone or laptop request some data from internet , what data we get actually comes from a web service..So client CPU will not be much occupied and less CPU occupancy will enhance battery life of our device.Hence , better overall performance of our phone.

eg… Playing high graphics , animated games or video from out phone memory will make our phone hang but if they will be played online than load on our CPU will be less.Data from web service will give faster response comparatively.
Database for a web service will be somewhere on cloud and can serve us better with faster response.

How to access Web Service in Android Studio
Different parsing methods r available to use web services like - XML parsing , Pull parsing , Json parsing. Apart from these parsing technique , we can also use 3rd party library to make use of web services. It makes networking easier and faster in android.
Some 3rd party libraries -

HTTP URL Connection - Also a HTTP library .have poor readability and less expressive.
Volley - 3rd party networking library. [HTTP library]
Retrofit - Uses Rest APIs. It’s interface uses HTTP_GET , HTTP_POST request. This library also handles http request and responses .
All these 3 libraries works asynchronously in background thread .This way they don’t use main thread and thus prevent long running operations from blocking the UI.

Volley’s Shortcomings
- Meager community to handle errors.
- Doesn’t have rest and other authentication features compare to retrofit.
- Have to explicitly convert json into desired object.
Retrofit - Active ,large community.So, easier troubleshooting.
- Expressive and concise code.
- Having built-in support to parse json using GSON library but volley doesn’t provide any automatic parsing of json data.
- We write less code as compared to Volley.Hence , less complex.

Types of data they can hold
VolleyRetrofit
String , Json Object , JsonArray , ImageRequestString ,Boolean ,Integer , Date ,object,collections

Volley Advantage
- It has a special type of request as ImageRequest to get images from network.while retrofit doesn’t support image loading directly but we can use Glide , picasso libraries with retrofit to load the image.
- Caching operation allowed not so with retrofit.

Image Loading Library

Various Image loading libraries - Glide ,Picasso , Universal Image Adapter
Image Adding through Picasso
Defining picture inside asset folder takes less memory than that of a drawable.

onCreate() { Image = findViewById(R.id.img); Picasso.with(this).load(R.drawable.apple).into(img); }

Change App Launcher Icon

Creating Bitmap and vector Image - These icons r present in the mipmap directory. To choose bitmap icon or vector image .
Go to - File ->new ->Image Asset
1.Image Asset -> Icon type ->Launcher icon
2.Image Asset ->Asset type ->Images
Choose 1 ->Next ->Finish
These bitmap . vector graphics r xml files. These graphical images can adjust automatically to different screen size and pixel density.

Min-SDK

Everytime we increase value of min-SDK , we reduced number of devices we can target. Default min-SDK is API-15.Hence , with this we can target 95% of devices that runs on google play store.So, to use more number of advance features , min-SDK value should be less.

Gradle Build script

Group all the configuration file in a single list.
Local.properties - Includes location of android SDK. On deleting this file , it gets recreated automatically.
Libs directory - We can add our Java Jar files here.

Manifest file

It describes the app to the OS and the application framework. All the activities ,services , we use in our app or the type of data we r passing , this files contains list of every such components.It's a config file having declaration of every activity , service or other components that we use in our app and contains all permissions and usable data which requires some access from the user's device that could be access to device memory , contact , internal files.

Components Architecture

Each activity and fragments has its own view model where we store data in memory wrap in data objects. Repository classes r where we store our actual data.
A view model stores persistent data in memory and manage business logic. It survive in changed configuration.eg… when activity switches from portrait to landscape and vice-versa.

Different Data persistent Technologies

Shared Preferences - It is a data storing mechanism which stored data in key-value pairs.It can store data of primitive type (int ,boolean ,char, float ,long) or object type (string ,string -array ). They store data persistently in uncrypted xml file inside internal storage.Key always use string instances.
Drawbacks - - They r not encrypted.Hence , not much safe to store password or credit card info.
- They can be deleted easily from the device.User can go into setting tab or app list to remove the data.
Keywords used - getPreferences() , getSharedPreferences();
If we want to store data having some primary key in rows and column format than SQLite DB is used to store table or column data.


SQLite DB - They store data in table format and provide permanent storage of our data and stores data to a text file on a device .
- Cursor points to every row and data fields of table.
- SQLite OpenHelper class used to update DB only when needed.
- SQLite DB name must end with .db
- ON CONFLICT REPLACE handle duplicate id insertion with sqlite.
- We use “?” in SQL selector query to help avoid SQL injection.
- rawQuery() of sqlite db returns a cursor over the result set.
Steps to retrieve info. Through cursor 1.Get column index by name.
2.Get column value by index.
ContentValues is used to update records in db table.

Use of DB Inspector
When we make app using SQLite DB than on running it , all the things that we store inside app will be reflected in the DB inspector.On clicking refresh table icon , we can view the changes to our app by using Live update icon.So, there will not be any need to refresh table again and again.


Room Library It’s an alternative way to persist data and deals with annotation.It’s an abstraction over SQLite. It allows compile time verification.
It’s components - 1.Entity - Represents class that hold info. About DB table.
2.DAO - Responsible for defining the methods that access the db.
3.Room DB- Main access point for underlying db connection.It also holds info about various entity and DAO.Room uses annotation like - @Entity,@PrimaryKey,@columnInfo,@Integer, @Dao(before interface or abstract class) ,@query(before placing any query) ,@Insert,@Update,@delete. Key points - - Can be static or dynamic.
- Uses raw SQL queries.
- Results returned as object not cursor.
Eg… @Query(“Select * from emp where active=1”) List getAll(); - Return boolean record as 0 or 1 rather than false or true.


Cupboard It is also a derivative of SQLite db to persist data.It works with android API , by contact provider,cursors and content values. It reduces numbe of line of code when compare with room and other storage technique.It is best to use with simple queries.


Realm Another way to persist data. It is a noSQL mobile DB .don’t rely on SQLite anyway.It also exposes data as object like incase of room and can make use of annotation.Define member and object as a normal class not through queries.It supports most primitive type.Realm transaction follows ACID property of DB. In realm , @required annotation is used to prevent null values from being persisted into memory.
@index to speed up queries against a certain field.

Add divider in recyclerView Programmetically

RecyclerView r ;
DividerItemDecoration d =new DividerItemDecoration (r.getContext(),layoutManager.getOrientation());
r.addItemDecoration(d);

addToBacktack()

While working with fragmemts , pressing back button directly makes exit from app.So, while we switch among fragments we want our app to return back to previous fragment by pressing back button. addToBackStack(null);

Use of Implicit Intent

Intent for starting camera Intent i = new Intent(MediaStore.INTENT_ACTION_IMAGE_CAMERA); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) != null) startActivity(i); //photo will be snapped but it’ll not pass back to our app , it’s just an operation of photo click.
Intent for Capturing Image Private static final int GET_IMAGE_CAPTURE = 1000; Intent i = new Intent(MediaStore.ACTION_IMAGE_CAPTURE); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) != null) startActivityForResult(i,GET_IMAGE_CAPTURE); protected void onActivityResult(int requestCode ,int resultcode,Intent data){ if(requestCode==GET_IMAGE_CAPTURE && resultcode ==RESULT_OK){ Bundle b =data.getExtras(); Bitmap bm = (Bitmap) b.get(“datas”); image.setImageBitmap(bm); } }
Intent to open a URL with browser String url =”http://www.google.com”; Uri webpage = Uri.parse(url); Intent i = new Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW ,webpage); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) != null) startActivity(i);
Intent to compose a SMS message String s =”This is a text message from Priyanka”; Intent i = new Intent(Intent.ACTION_SENDTO); i.setData(Uri.parse(“sms : 1888555231 “); i.putExtra(“sms body” ,s); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) != null) startActivity(i);
Intent to set an Alarm String m =”This is a sample from priyanka”; final int hour=6; final int min = 30; Intent i = new Intent(AlarmClock.ACTION_SET_ALARM).putExtra(AlarmClock.EXTRA_MESSAGE,m) .putExtra(AlarmClock.EXTRA_HOUR,hour).putExtra(AlarmClock.EXTRA_MINUTES,min) .putExtra(AlarmClock.EXTRA_VIBRATE,true) ; if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) != null ) startActivity(i); Manifest.xml -
Intent to perform a web search String s = “Eiffel Tower”; Intent i = new Intent(Intent.ACTION_WEB_SEARCH); i.putExtra(SearchManager.QUERY , s); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager())!= null ) startActivity(i);
Intent to open a phone dialer String num = “0-889-556-8888”; Uri n = Uri.parse(“tel : “ + num); Intent i = new Intent(Intent.ACTION_DIAL); i.setData(n); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) != null) startActivity(i); Manifest.xml Intent.ACTION_DIAL will not make any call in actual .It will just open the dialer. To make call use Intent.ACTION_CALL.
Intent to compose an Email String [] addrss ={“priya32@gmail.com”}; String[] cc ={“pnigam67@gmail.com” , “chi25@gmail.com” } ; Sting msg =”This is a test mail “; String sub = “Ckecking email” ; Intent i = new Intent(Intent.ACTION_SENDTO); i.setData(Uri.parse(“mailto:”)); i.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_EMAIL , addrss); i.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_SUBJECT , sub); i.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_CC , cc); i.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_TEXT , msg); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) != null) startActivity(i);
Intent to show location on a map String loc = “geo:0,0 ?q=20+w+34th+st+10001”; Uri u =Uri.parse(loc); Intent i = new Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW , u); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) !=null ) startActivity(i);
Intent to show how app can show an app chooser to display available apps String m =”This is test sample”; Intent i =new Intent(); i.setAction(Intent.ACTION_SEND); i.setType(“text/plain”): i.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_TEXT , m ); Intent chooser = Intent.createChooser(i,”Select an App”); if(i.resolveActivity(getPackageManager()) !=null ) startActivity(i);

Pending Intent

It’s a wrapper around existing intent. They r use to grab the ability to fire an intent to another app as if it is executing from our app process. Eg… Notification App . When we create a notification in our app and attach out intent to it to tell android which activity should be started when a user tap on that notification.

Toast and SnackBar

Toast bar is just asimple message in the form of acknowledgement but they don’t have any optional action that user can interact with.But Snackbar does provides some interaction.Here to set an action , setAction() is used.But they both r passive user communication element.SNackbar can be simply removed by swiping-off.We can have the flexibility to add other action inside snackbar.

Public void onClick(View v){ Snackbar.make(v,”Replace”,Snackbar.LENGTH_LONG).setAction(“click me”,new View.OnClickListener(){ Public void onClick(View v){ do(); } }).show(); }}); }
Public void do(){ Toast.makeText(this, “u clicked here”,Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show(); }}

View Pager

It’s a component to allow user to swipe through dataset seeing 1 data element at a time.It’s an alternative to listview but let each item occupy entire screen.For ViewPager , we use Fragments.

Resolving Some common error of android Studio

1.Cannot fit requested classes in a single dex file -> Add “multiDexEnabled true” in app level gradle inside Defaultconfig { minSDkVersion 16 targetSDKVersion 31 testinstrumentationRunner multiDexEnabled true }

2. Task : app : checkDebugAarmetadata failed eror -> Add runtimeOnly(group : ‘androidx.browser’ , name : ‘browser’ , version : ‘1.3.0’ ) {force =true} runtimeOnly(group : ‘androidx.appompat’, version : ‘1.3.1’) {force=true}

3.Resource not found exception -> 1 reason is occurence of some integer value in view.setText() .eg… textView.setText(x) //will give this error for x=8 So, give it like - textView.setText(“ “+x) ;

4.Java.lang.IlllegalStateException - -> use a Theme,Appcompattheme (or its descendants) in listView.setOnclickListener( ALertDailog.Builder(AlertDialog.Builder(getApplicationContext()); Change this to listView.setOnclickListener( ALertDailog.Builder(AlertDialog.Builder(this);

5.App couldn’t install ->Installation didn’t succeed 1.Delete debug folder and try to rebuild project. 2.File ->Cache invalidate and restart. 3.Manifest merger failed -> Use only permisssion & libraries that r required in the app , remove else. Frequently Asked Questions on Android Studio

What is Android Studio?

Android Studio is the official IDE for Android application development provided by Google.

Which programming languages are used in Android Studio?

Android Studio mainly supports Java and Kotlin for Android application development.

What is Gradle in Android Studio?

Gradle is the build automation tool used in Android Studio for dependency management and application builds.

What is an APK file?

APK stands for Android Package Kit. It is the installable package file format for Android applications.

What is Logcat in Android Studio?

Logcat is a debugging tool that displays system logs and application logs during app execution.

What is Android Emulator?

Android Emulator is a virtual Android device used for testing applications without a physical device.

What is Android SDK?

Android SDK is a collection of development tools, libraries and APIs required for Android app development.

What is ADB in Android Studio?

ADB (Android Debug Bridge) is a command-line tool used to communicate with Android devices and emulators.

What is the use of AndroidManifest.xml?

AndroidManifest.xml contains important information about the app such as permissions, activities and services.

What are Activities in Android?

Activities represent a single screen with a user interface in an Android application.

What are Fragments in Android?

Fragments are reusable portions of UI and behavior that can be combined within activities.

What is RecyclerView in Android?

RecyclerView is a flexible and efficient view used for displaying large sets of data in Android applications.

What is Intent in Android?

Intent is a messaging object used to start activities, services or communicate between Android components.

What is the difference between Explicit and Implicit Intent?

Explicit Intent directly specifies the target component, whereas Implicit Intent allows Android to choose a suitable component.

What is the difference between LinearLayout and ConstraintLayout?

LinearLayout arranges views in a single direction, while ConstraintLayout provides flexible positioning using constraints.

What is Toast in Android?

Toast is a small popup notification used to display short messages to users.

What is ANR in Android?

ANR stands for Application Not Responding. It occurs when the app becomes unresponsive for a long time.

What is ProGuard in Android?

ProGuard is a tool used to shrink, optimize and obfuscate Android application code.

What is Firebase in Android development?

Firebase is a Google platform that provides backend services such as authentication, database, analytics and cloud messaging.

What is the difference between Debug and Release APK?

Debug APK is used for testing and development, whereas Release APK is optimized and signed for production deployment.
